course_info.title: Foreign Language II
course_info.meeting_schedule: Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday - 1 hour per session

class_schedule.topic: Review and Expansion: Building on LANG201 Foundations
learning_objectives: Review and consolidate present and past tense skills
Expand vocabulary for professional and academic contexts
Set goals for intermediate communicative competence
readings: Selected authentic media in the target language

lecture_notes.introduction: LANG202 builds on the foundations of LANG201 to develop intermediate communicative competence. You will move from survival language to genuine engagement with ideas.
key_concepts: Review: present and past tense, basic vocabulary, fundamental structures
Expanding to professional and academic vocabulary
Introduction to authentic materials: news, podcasts, short articles
Self-assessment: identifying strengths and areas for improvement
Goals for intermediate competence: extended discourse, nuance, argumentation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Self-assessment exercise: recording a spontaneous monologue and evaluating fluency, accuracy, and range

review.value: The ability to handle most routine situations, narrate and describe in connected discourse, and express opinions with supporting reasons -- moving beyond survival phrases to genuine communication
Real-world texts, audio, and video produced for native speakers rather than textbook learners -- essential for developing genuine comprehension skills

class_schedule.topic: Future Tense and Expressing Plans and Intentions
learning_objectives: Form and use future tense constructions
Express intentions using appropriate modal structures

lecture_notes.introduction: Adding the future tense to your repertoire allows you to discuss plans, make predictions, and express intentions -- essential for professional communication.
key_concepts: Future tense formation: regular and irregular patterns
Expressing plans: going to vs. will (or equivalent structures)
Modal verbs: can, must, should, want to in the target language
Making predictions and expressing probability
Cultural note: attitudes toward time, punctuality, and planning
lecture_notes.demonstration: Role-play: discussing a study abroad plan or career goals using future tense and modals

review.value: The grammatical structures for expressing future actions, which vary widely across languages: some use verb endings, others use auxiliary verbs, and many have multiple future constructions
Verbs expressing ability, obligation, possibility, or desire (can, must, should, want) that add nuance and are essential for professional-level communication

learning_objectives: Read simplified news articles in the target language
Develop strategies for reading comprehension with authentic texts
readings: Simplified news articles from target language media

lecture_notes.introduction: Reading authentic texts develops vocabulary, grammar intuition, and cultural knowledge simultaneously. News reading connects language study directly to international studies.
key_concepts: Reading strategies: skimming, scanning, context clues, cognates
News vocabulary: politics, economics, society, environment
Summarizing: extracting and restating main ideas in your own words
Discussion skills: asking follow-up questions, responding to peers
Media literacy in the target language: source evaluation
lecture_notes.demonstration: Jigsaw reading: groups read different articles and summarize for each other in the target language

review.value: Techniques for comprehending texts beyond your current level: using context clues, cognates, structural knowledge, and tolerance of ambiguity rather than translating word by word
Specialized vocabulary for discussing politics, economics, and international affairs in the target language -- directly useful for international studies professionals

class_schedule.topic: Subjunctive Mood and Expressing Wishes, Doubts, and Emotions
learning_objectives: Understand and form the subjunctive mood (or equivalent)
Express wishes, doubts, recommendations, and emotions
Use complex sentence structures with subordinate clauses

lecture_notes.introduction: The subjunctive mood allows you to express wishes, doubts, emotions, and hypothetical situations -- adding crucial nuance to your communication.
key_concepts: Subjunctive formation: triggers and conjugation patterns
Expressing wishes and desires using subjunctive structures
Expressing doubt and uncertainty
Recommendations and suggestions: polite and indirect forms
Complex sentences: main clause + subordinate clause
lecture_notes.demonstration: Role-play: diplomatic negotiation scene using subjunctive for polite suggestions and hypothetical proposals

review.value: A verb form used to express wishes, doubts, emotions, and hypothetical situations that differs significantly from English (where subjunctive is mostly lost) in Romance, Germanic, and other language families
Sentences with main and subordinate clauses connected by conjunctions (that, because, although, when, if) enabling more sophisticated expression of ideas and relationships

class_schedule.topic: Professional Communication: Formal Writing and Business Correspondence
learning_objectives: Write formal emails and business letters in the target language
Use appropriate register and formality markers
Draft a professional document: cover letter or formal inquiry
readings: Sample professional correspondence in the target language

lecture_notes.introduction: Professional communication in another language requires mastery of formal register, specific conventions, and cultural expectations that differ from casual speech.
key_concepts: Formal writing conventions: salutations, closings, tone
Email etiquette in the target culture
Business letter format and standard phrases
Making requests, inquiries, and complaints formally
Cultural note: business communication norms in the target culture
lecture_notes.demonstration: Writing workshop: drafting a formal email requesting information about a study abroad program

review.value: Written professional communication uses different vocabulary, grammar, and conventions than spoken language, with culture-specific formulas for openings, closings, and requests
Understanding that business communication norms (directness, hierarchy, relationship-building, decision-making) vary significantly across cultures and are reflected in language use

class_schedule.topic: Debate and Discussion: Engaging with Complex Ideas
learning_objectives: Participate in structured debates on international topics
Express complex arguments with supporting evidence
readings: Selected opinion pieces in the target language

lecture_notes.introduction: The ability to discuss complex ideas, express nuanced opinions, and engage with others' arguments in another language is the mark of true communicative competence.
key_concepts: Debate structure: proposition, argument, evidence, rebuttal
Expressing complex opinions with qualification and nuance
Using linking words for argumentation: however, therefore, despite, although
Responding to counterarguments respectfully
Self-assessment: how far have you come since LANG201?
lecture_notes.demonstration: Formal debate on an international topic conducted entirely in the target language

review.value: The ability to construct and defend arguments with evidence, use logical connectors, and respond to counterpoints -- the highest-value language skill for international studies professionals
Connectors like however, therefore, although, despite, in addition, on the other hand that structure arguments and are essential for coherent extended discourse

quizzes.topic: Future Tense, Modals, and News Reading

questions.question: Write 5-6 sentences in the target language describing your career goals after graduation. Use at least 3 different future tense constructions and 2 modal verbs.
questions.answer: Response should include 5-6 grammatically correct sentences about career goals using at least 3 correctly formed future tense constructions and 2 modal verbs, demonstrating ability to discuss plans and intentions.

questions.question: Read the provided short news article in the target language and write a 4-5 sentence summary in the target language. Your summary should capture the main idea, key facts, and significance.
questions.answer: Summary should accurately capture the main idea, include at least 2 key facts, and indicate the significance of the event. Language should be the student's own words, not copied from the text.

quizzes.topic: Subjunctive, Complex Sentences, and Professional Writing

questions.question: Write a formal email (8-10 sentences) in the target language to the director of an international organization, expressing interest in an internship. Include a self-introduction, your qualifications, and a polite request for information. Use appropriate formal register.
questions.answer: Email should include appropriate formal opening and closing, self-introduction with relevant qualifications, polite request using subjunctive or conditional forms, and consistent formal register throughout.

questions.question: Write 4-5 sentences in the target language expressing your opinion on a global issue (climate change, migration, or trade). Use at least 2 subjunctive constructions and 2 linking words for argumentation.
questions.answer: Response should express a clear opinion on a global issue with supporting reasoning, using at least 2 subjunctive constructions and 2 argumentation linking words (however, therefore, although, etc.).

major_assignments.title: International Studies Research Presentation in Target Language
major_assignments.description: Select a topic from your international studies coursework and deliver a 10-minute research presentation entirely in the target language, demonstrating intermediate communicative competence and cultural awareness.
objectives: Deliver a sustained presentation on an academic topic in the target language
Demonstrate intermediate speaking fluency and accuracy
Show ability to handle questions and discussion in the target language
Integrate language skills with international studies content knowledge
deliverables: 10-minute oral presentation in the target language
Presentation slides in the target language
1000-word written summary in the target language

criteria: Handles audience questions in the target language
Effective slides in the target language

weekly_quizzes.description: Bi-weekly quizzes on grammar, vocabulary, and communicative tasks

daily_participation.description: Active target language use in every class session

major_assignments.description: Research presentation and written summary in the target language

midterm_examination.description: Written and oral exam covering Units 6-8

final_examination.description: Comprehensive written and oral exam covering all units, including a debate component

course_policies.attendance: Attendance is critical. More than 3 unexcused absences reduce final grade by one letter step. More than 6 result in automatic failure.
course_policies.late_submissions: Late written work penalized 5% per day. Presentations cannot be rescheduled without medical documentation.
course_policies.collaboration: Pair and group work in class is expected. Individual assessments must reflect your own ability.
course_policies.academic_integrity: Translation software and AI generators may not be used for graded work. Dictionaries and grammar references are permitted.
course_policies.technology_requirements: Access to target-language media, language learning platform, and presentation software.
